siteTitle: Ozly - Unlimited Invoices & Taxes Under Control

hero.title: No more mess. Shifts, Invoices & Taxes in one place.
hero.titleHighlight: Built for ABN and/or TFN workers in Australia.
hero.subtitle: Schedule Jobs, issue and manage your invoices, forecast your revenue, track and organise tax-deductible expenses, estimate how much tax you'll owe this year, check your working hours compliance, and much more — all in one app! Starting from {price}.

audience.title: Built by immigrants, for immigrants and students working as freelancers in Australia

realEarnings.title: That $12 delivery…
realEarnings.titleHighlight: how much did you really earn?
realEarnings.subtitle: Most people only see the payment. Ozly shows you what's left after the real costs.
realEarnings.bottomLine: Whether it's a delivery, a cleaning job, or a weekend shift — Ozly tracks every expense so you always know your true hourly rate.

features.title: Everything ABN & TFN workers need,
features.subtitle: Stop juggling 4 different apps. Ozly handles shifts, invoices, tax, expenses, penalty rates and salary forecasting in one place.
features.shiftsDesc: Import Jobs from Google Calendar or create Jobs with flexible duration, frequency, and hourly rate. Issue invoices and send to contractors in seconds, track and chase overdue invoices.
features.ocrDesc: Snap a photo of receipts to claim tax-deductible expenses — the app stores and organises the photos, reads each item and estimates your deduction. Use Quick Mode to log with a single tap.
features.trackerDesc: See your gross pay, income tax withholding and net pay in real time. Automatic penalty rate calculations for weekends and public holidays, based on your industry.
features.forecastDesc: Compare your ABN income vs TFN salary to negotiate better terms with your employer. Know how much to set aside for your Tax Return and how much Superannuation you're entitled to.

comparison.title: Ozly
comparison.subtitle: Everything you need to work for yourself in Australia — no spreadsheets, no red tape, no headaches. Track your work, invoice your clients and know what's left in your pocket — all in one app.

pricing.title: Choose Your Plan
pricing.subtitle: No surprises, no per-invoice fee, no percentage of your earnings. All plans include a 14-day free trial.
pricing.annualNote: * Annual plan: TFN/ABN $149.99/yr | PRO $199.99/yr. All plans include a 14-day free trial.
pricing.calendarNote: *** Currently integrates with Google Calendar. Compatible with apps that sync with Google Calendar.

homeFaq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
homeFaq.subtitle: Quick answers to the most common questions about Ozly.
homeFaq.stillQuestionsDesc: Get in touch or explore our complete guide with step-by-step instructions for every feature.

guide.title: Complete User Guide & FAQ
guide.subtitle: Everything you need to know to use Ozly and organise your jobs, invoices and finances in Australia.
guide.contactSubtitle: Send us an email and we'll get back to you quickly.

bottomCta.title: While you're reading this, someone is sending an Invoice through Ozly.
bottomCta.subtitle: Download the app and get started now.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.summary: Ozly is a workforce-management and expense-tracking application designed for Australian contractors and small businesses. We collect only the data necessary to deliver our services. Your financial data is encrypted on your device, and you may request complete deletion of your account and all associated data at any time.
privacy.s8: 8. Your Rights Under Australian Privacy Law

terms.title: Terms of Use (EULA)
terms.importantText: By downloading, installing, or using the Ozly application, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree to these terms, do not use the App.

faq.pageSubtitle: Find answers to common questions about Ozly. Can't find what you need? Get in touch.
faq.contactSubtitle: Send us an email and we'll get back to you quickly.
faq.noResults: No results. Try different keywords or contact support.
faq.a1: Ozly is an app built for anyone working with an ABN and/or TFN in Australia — Cleaning, Aged & Childcare, Hospitality and any Sole Trader. Register your employers and shifts, generate unlimited invoices, log your expenses in seconds to claim on your tax return, and get a revenue forecast so you stay financially organised even on the go.
faq.a2: Yes! Ozly works 100% offline. You can create invoices, log expenses and track your jobs without wifi. Everything syncs automatically when you're back online.
faq.q3: How does the smart camera for expenses work?
faq.a3: With Ozly's smart camera, just take a photo of your receipt and in seconds the app reads each item and all the data from the note, letting you select the ones that are work-related expenses, therefore deductible on your income tax (Tax return). The app stores the receipts and totals everything, estimating how much tax you'll owe at the end of the financial year.
faq.q4: Does the app calculate my tax?
faq.a4: Yes. Ozly estimates how much tax you'll owe following ATO (Australian Taxation Office) rules. No surprises at tax return time.
faq.q5: Does Ozly integrate with Google Calendar?
faq.a5: Yes, Ozly syncs with your Google Calendar, importing Jobs registered there. Handy when your employer schedules your shifts in Google Calendar. Just tap the calendar icon in the top corner of the main screen.
faq.q6: How does Ozly help with invoice management?
faq.a6: With Ozly, you not only generate your invoice in a professional standard template, but you can also send it via WhatsApp, email or even SMS to the contractor, all in seconds, without needing a bunch of spreadsheets!
faq.q7: Is Ozly available in other languages?
faq.a7: Yes! Ozly is available in Portuguese, English and Spanish, both in the app and on the website.
faq.a8: Visa Shield monitors your hours worked per fortnight (last 14 rolling days) to help you stay below the limit allowed for working and student visas.
faq.a9: The Hustle Score is a gamification system that rewards you with XP for completing jobs, creating invoices, logging expenses and maintaining streaks. You progress through 4 tiers: Starter, Hustler, Pro and Legend.
faq.a10: Yes. Your data is encrypted on your device and protected on the server. Ozly follows industry best practices to safeguard your personal and financial information.
faq.q11: How does the referral program work?
faq.a11: Share your referral code with friends. When they sign up using your code and after one month on a paid plan, you both get 15 free days. You also earn 500 XP for each converted referral.
faq.q12: What is the difference between TFN and ABN mode?
faq.a12: TFN mode is designed for employees (Tax File Number) — it shows net salary after PAYG withholding, super contributions, and doesn't require invoices. ABN mode is for contractors with an Australian Business Number — it includes invoicing, contractor management, and GST tracking. Pro plan gives you both.
faq.a13: Ozly automatically calculates penalty rates based on your industry (Cleaning, Hospitality, Retail, Construction, Transport, Healthcare, and more). Weekend and public holiday shifts get automatic multipliers on your base rate. It also knows all Australian public holidays by state.
faq.q14: Can I enter my salary as annual or fortnightly?
faq.a14: Yes! TFN users can enter their salary as Hourly, Annual, Monthly, or Fortnightly. Ozly automatically converts it to an hourly rate based on a standard 38-hour week and shows your estimated gross, PAYG and net take-home pay.
faq.a15: Quick Expense mode lets you log an expense with just the amount, category and date — no receipt photo needed. Smart mode uses AI OCR to read your receipt and extract all items automatically. You choose which mode suits each expense.
faq.q16: Can I bulk edit or delete multiple jobs?
faq.a16: Yes! Long-press any job to enter multi-select mode. You can then select multiple jobs and bulk edit the hourly rate or delete them all at once. Great for managing recurring shifts.
faq.a17: Ozly generates professional PDF timesheets from your completed jobs. You can filter by employer/contractor, and the timesheet includes dates, hours, rates, and totals. Share it directly via email or WhatsApp.
faq.a18: TFN and Pro users can log work-related expenses for reimbursement (fuel, laundry, tools, cleaning supplies, transport, uniform, phone, equipment). Use Smart Mode to scan a receipt with AI or Quick Mode to enter details manually. Select a period (weekly, fortnightly, monthly), choose a category, and save. You can then select multiple reimbursements, generate a professional PDF with your bank details, and send it to your employer via email or WhatsApp.
faq.q19: How do I export reimbursement PDFs?
faq.a19: Go to Reimbursements, tap the checklist icon to enter select mode, pick the items you want, and tap the PDF icon. Review your personal details, invoice number and bank info, then generate the PDF. You can send it via Email (with receipt photos attached), WhatsApp, or save it to your device. Items are automatically marked as 'emitted' after generating the PDF.
faq.q20: How does the referral program link work?
faq.a20: Each user gets a unique referral code. Share your personal link (ozly.au/refer/?code=YOUR_CODE) with friends. When they open it on their phone, the page automatically detects whether they're on iOS or Android and shows the right download button. They enter your code during signup, and you both earn rewards plus 500 XP.
faq.q21: Can I customise the email messages for invoices and reimbursements?
faq.a21: Yes! Go to Settings and find Message Templates. You can edit three templates: Invoice Send, Invoice Reminder, and Reimbursement. Each template supports placeholders like {name}, {number}, {amount} and {date} that are filled in automatically when you send. You can reset to defaults any time.
faq.q22: How do I cancel my subscription?
faq.a22: You can cancel anytime directly through the App Store or Google Play. No hassle, no cancellation fee. To cancel within the app, go to Menu → Settings → Account → Cancel subscription. If you also want to permanently delete your account and all data, tap Delete My Account.
faq.q23: What happened to the Max plan?
faq.a23: The Max plan has been renamed to Pro — same features, same price, same combined ABN + TFN access. If you already had Max, your subscription was kept and works as Pro automatically. You don't need to do anything.
faq.q24: What is the Pro Consolidated Dashboard view?
faq.a24: Pro users get a 3-way toggle at the top of the Dashboard: All / ABN / TFN. In the All view, a Consolidated Card sums your paid ABN invoices plus your forecasted TFN net salary into a single total, with a percentage breakdown by source. Useful when you wear both hats and want to see your full picture at a glance.
faq.q25: How does the Quick Actions FAB on the Dashboard work?
faq.a25: The floating + button on the Dashboard opens a quick-action sheet adapted to your active mode. ABN users see Add Job, Add Expense and Create Invoice. TFN users see Add Shift, Add Expense and Add Reimbursement. Pro users get all of them. It's the fastest way to log new entries from anywhere on the dashboard.
faq.q26: What is the Rate Comparison feature?
faq.a26: Rate Comparison (a Pro-only feature) shows how your hourly rate compares to peers in your sector and state. When at least 10 peers exist in the same bucket, you see real Ozly peer data with your percentile. Otherwise, Ozly falls back to public market data sourced from the Fair Work Commission, ABS and PayScale. You'll find it as a chip on the Jobs screen and as a card on the Financial screen.
faq.q27: Does Ozly give me tax or legal advice?
faq.a27: No. Ozly is an organisational and educational tool. All calculations — PAYG, GST, super, deductions, Visa Shield, Net Forecast — are estimates only and do not constitute professional tax, financial or legal advice. Always consult a registered tax agent or accountant before making decisions, and contact the Department of Home Affairs for authoritative visa rules. You'll see legal disclaimers on critical screens (Visa Shield, Rate Comparison, Financial, Paywall) and a full breakdown under Settings → Legal Disclaimers.
faq.q28: I have a working/student visa. Is Visa Shield official?
faq.a28: No. Visa Shield is for tracking only. It estimates your fortnightly hours so you can stay aware of your visa limit, but it is not affiliated with the Department of Home Affairs and does not represent official compliance. You are solely responsible for following the rules of your visa subclass. For authoritative limits, always check the Department of Home Affairs or your visa sponsor.
faq.q29: How does the auto-renewal of the subscription work?
faq.a29: All paid plans auto-renew monthly through Apple's App Store or Google Play, depending on the device used to subscribe. Ozly does not store or process payment details — Apple and Google handle billing entirely. To change or cancel your plan, manage it in your store's subscription settings. The 14-day free trial does not auto-charge if cancelled before it ends.
faq.q30: Where do I find all the legal disclaimers in one place?
faq.a30: Open the app and go to Settings → Legal Disclaimers. You'll find four sections: (1) No Professional Advice — Ozly does not replace an accountant or lawyer; (2) Accuracy Limitations — calculations are estimates; (3) Liability Limitations — Ozly is not liable for direct or indirect damages; (4) Third-party Services — payment is processed by Apple and Google, not by Ozly. The same content is available in English, Portuguese and Spanish.
faq.q31: How do I sign up for Ozly?
faq.a31: Open the app, tap Sign Up, and enter your email and a password (minimum 6 characters; we recommend at least one uppercase letter and one number). Confirm the password — a green check appears when both match. Tap Sign Up and you'll be taken to the Setup screen to configure your profile.
faq.q32: Can I log in with Google or Apple?
faq.a32: Yes. On the login screen, tap Continue with Google or Continue with Apple. You'll be asked to authorise the app and then taken straight to the Dashboard. If it's your first login, you go to the Setup screen first to fill in your basic info.
faq.q33: I forgot my password. How do I reset it?
faq.a33: On the login screen tap Forgot Password?, enter your email, and tap Reset. You'll get an email with a recovery link — open it and create a new password. To change your password later, go to Profile → Change Password (only available for accounts created with email/password).
faq.q34: Can I use Ozly without internet?
faq.a34: Yes. Once you've logged in at least once with internet, Ozly caches your session and works fully offline. You can create jobs, log expenses and view your data. An orange Offline mode banner appears at the top. When you reconnect, everything syncs automatically.
faq.q35: How do I permanently delete my account?
faq.a35: Go to Settings → Account → Delete My Account. You'll get a confirmation dialog warning that all your data (jobs, invoices, expenses, contractors) will be permanently removed and cannot be recovered. After confirming, your account is wiped from Ozly servers within 30 days, in line with GDPR/LGPD compliance.
faq.q36: What information do I need to enter during Setup?
faq.a36: ABN users: full name, postal code, ABN (11 digits), business name, category (Cleaning, Hospitality, etc.) and bank details (BSB + account or PayID). TFN users: full name, postal code, visa type and TFN (optional). Pro users fill both. Email is taken from your auth — you don't re-enter it.
faq.q37: How do I add a profile photo?
faq.a37: On the Setup screen or in Profile, tap the avatar circle. You can take a new photo with the camera or pick one from your gallery. The image is automatically resized and saved with your profile.
faq.q38: Can I have multiple businesses (more than one ABN)?
faq.a38: Yes. Pro users can register multiple ABNs. Go to Profile → Businesses → Add New Business. Fill in the new ABN, company name and category. You can switch the active business at any time, and each business has its own jobs, invoices and contractors.
faq.q39: What do the cards on the Dashboard show?
faq.a39: The Dashboard summarises your activity for the selected period: earnings, hours worked, expenses, next job and a tax forecast. The exact cards depend on your mode (ABN, TFN or Pro). For example, TFN users see Net Salary Forecast, ABN users see Financial Hero (invoices) and Pro users can see all of them via the All toggle.
faq.q40: How does the period selector work?
faq.a40: At the top of the Dashboard you'll find Day / Week / Month / Custom. Tap any option to filter all the cards to that period. Custom lets you pick a specific date range. The selection persists across sessions so the dashboard always opens on your preferred view.
faq.q41: How do I read the tax forecast?
faq.a41: The forecast estimates how much tax you'll owe at the end of the financial year based on your current earnings and expenses, following ATO progressive tax brackets and Medicare levy rules. It updates as you add jobs and expenses. Remember: it's an estimate — always confirm with a registered tax agent before lodging.
faq.q42: How do I see the tutorial again?
faq.a42: Go to Settings → General → Replay Walkthrough. Return to the Dashboard and the interactive tutorial reappears with overlays explaining each main feature.
faq.q43: How do I create a job (or shift)?
faq.a43: Tap the floating + button on the Dashboard or go to Jobs → +. Fill in: title, contractor (or use a quick preset like Uber Eats, DoorDash, Cleaning), date, start/end time (or pick a duration chip — 1h, 2h, 4h, 7.5h Full Day) and hourly rate or total. Tap Save. The job appears in your Jobs list immediately.
faq.q44: How do I edit, complete or delete a job?
faq.a44: On the Jobs screen, tap a job to open its details. Edit any field and Save. To complete, tap Complete — a celebration sheet appears with hours, earnings and XP. To delete, swipe or use the menu — you'll be asked to confirm.
faq.q45: Can I add a job in the past?
faq.a45: Yes. When creating a job, simply pick a past date in the date picker — there's no restriction. This is useful for backfilling shifts you forgot to log.
faq.q46: How do I save a job as a template?
faq.a46: When creating or editing a job, tap the bookmark/template icon and give it a name (e.g. Tuesday Cleaning). Next time, tap Templates on the Add Job screen and pick the saved template — all fields fill in automatically.
faq.q47: How do I set up recurring jobs?
faq.a47: On the Add Job screen, toggle Recurring and choose Weekly, Fortnightly, Monthly or Custom. Pick the end date or number of repetitions. Ozly creates each occurrence as an individual job, so you can edit or complete each one separately.
faq.a48: Quick Add reuses your last job's contractor, time and duration so you can add the next job in two taps. It's perfect for repetitive shifts where only the date changes.
faq.q49: How do I filter or search my jobs?
faq.a49: On the Jobs screen, tap the filter icon to filter by status (Pending, Confirmed, Completed, Cancelled), contractor or date range. The search bar at the top searches by title or contractor name.
faq.a50: Pending — created but not yet confirmed. Confirmed — scheduled and ready. Completed — finished, earnings counted. Cancelled — won't happen, doesn't count toward earnings or hours.
faq.q51: How do I add a contractor (employer)?
faq.a51: Go to Contractors → +. Fill in the name (the only required field), and optionally email, phone, ABN, address and default rate. You can also create one inline when adding a job by tapping + next to the contractor field.
faq.q52: What's the difference between Agency and Direct Client?
faq.a52: Agency is a labour-hire company that places you with end clients (e.g. a cleaning agency). Direct Client is the end client you work for directly. Use the tabs in the Contractors list to filter between them. The distinction helps when generating timesheets and invoices.
faq.q53: Can I call, message or email a contractor from the app?
faq.a53: Yes. On the contractor card, tap the phone, SMS, email or WhatsApp icon — the system opens the relevant app pre-filled with the contact details so you can get in touch in one tap.
faq.q54: Can I import a contact from my phone?
faq.a54: Yes. On the Add Contractor screen, tap Import from Contacts and select a contact from your phone book. The name, phone and email fill in automatically.
faq.q55: How do I create an invoice?
faq.a55: Go to Financial → Invoices → + or, after completing a job, tap Generate Invoice on the celebration sheet. Select the jobs to include — Ozly auto-fills hours, rate and totals. Customise the invoice number, date, due date and payment terms. Tap Generate PDF and the professional invoice is ready to send.
faq.q56: When does GST get added to my invoices?
faq.a56: GST (10%) is automatically suggested when your annual revenue exceeds AUD 75,000 (the ATO threshold for mandatory GST registration). You can enable/disable it manually for individual invoices in Settings → Tax.
faq.q57: How do I send an invoice?
faq.a57: After generating the PDF, tap the share icon. Choose Email (with the PDF attached), WhatsApp, SMS or Print. The default subject and body come from your message templates (editable in Settings → Message Templates).
faq.q58: How do I mark an invoice as paid? What are the statuses?
faq.a58: Open the invoice and tap Mark as Paid. Status lifecycle: Draft (still editing) → Sent (delivered to contractor) → Paid (received) or Overdue (past due date, not paid). Overdue is set automatically once the due date passes.
faq.q59: Can I send several invoices at once (batch send)?
faq.a59: Yes. On the Invoices screen, tap the select-multiple icon, choose the invoices and tap the email icon. All selected PDFs go out in a single email per contractor.
faq.a60: Only invoices in Draft status can be deleted (they haven't been sent yet). Once sent, you can mark as paid or void, but you can't delete it — that keeps your records consistent for tax purposes.
faq.q61: Which expense categories does Ozly use?
faq.a61: Ozly uses 9 ATO-aligned categories for sole traders: Vehicle (fuel, rego, repairs), Supplies, Tools, Uniform/PPE, Phone, Internet, Insurance, Travel and Other. Each category maps to the right section of your tax return.
faq.q62: How do I view or download a receipt photo?
faq.a62: Tap an expense in the list to open it, then tap the photo. You can pinch to zoom, or tap the download icon at the top to save it to your gallery or share it via WhatsApp/email.
faq.q63: What's the difference between Total and Claimable on an expense?
faq.a63: Total is the full amount on the receipt. Claimable is the work-related portion — for example, if you bought a phone bill of $80 and 70% is for work, the claimable amount is $56. Only the claimable amount counts toward your tax deduction.
faq.q64: Can I export my expenses as CSV?
faq.a64: Yes. On the Expenses screen, tap the export icon and choose CSV. The export contains 12 columns (date, merchant, total, claimable, category, description, etc.) — perfect for sharing with your accountant or importing into Excel.
faq.q65: When does the Australian financial year start?
faq.a65: The Australian financial year (FY) runs from 1 July to 30 June. Ozly calculates all tax forecasts and reports based on this period. You can see which FY you're in on the Fiscal screen.
faq.q66: When do I have to register for GST?
faq.a66: You must register for GST if your business turnover is AUD 75,000 or more in a 12-month period. Below that, registration is optional. Ozly automatically suggests enabling GST when you cross the threshold.
faq.q67: Which tax brackets does Ozly use?
faq.a67: Ozly uses the current ATO progressive brackets for residents (FY 2025–26): $0–$18,200: 0%; $18,201–$45,000: 16%; $45,001–$135,000: 30%; $135,001–$190,000: 37%; $190,001+: 45%. Plus a 2% Medicare levy when applicable (exempt for WHM and student visas). Working Holiday Makers use a separate 15% flat rate up to $45,000.
faq.q68: How do I export the fiscal report PDF?
faq.a68: Go to Fiscal → Export → PDF. The report includes a summary of paid invoices, claimable expenses, net income and estimated tax for the selected financial year. Share via email or save to your device.
faq.q69: I'm on a Working Holiday Visa (WHM). Does Ozly handle the special tax rules?
faq.a69: Yes. WHM holders are taxed at a flat 15% on the first $45,000 (and standard brackets above that). Set your visa type to Work Visa (WHM 417/462) in Profile and Ozly automatically applies the WHM rules. Medicare levy is exempt for WHM.
faq.q70: How does Visa Shield calculate my fortnightly hours?
faq.a70: Visa Shield uses a rolling 14-day window — at any moment, it shows how many hours you've worked in the last 14 calendar days, not in a fixed pay period. The default limit is 48 hours (the standard for Subclass 500 students). As you approach the limit, the bar turns yellow and then red.
faq.a71: Pending — logged but not yet sent to your employer. Emitted — included in a generated PDF and sent. Paid — confirmed paid by your employer. You can change the status manually as your employer processes the reimbursement.
faq.q72: How do I mark a reimbursement as paid?
faq.a72: On the Reimbursements screen, tap the reimbursement and tap Mark as Paid, or use bulk-select for several at once. The Fiscal screen automatically reflects paid reimbursements.
faq.q73: How do I switch between Light, Dark and Auto themes?
faq.a73: Settings → Appearance → Theme. Choose Light, Dark or Auto (follows your phone's system theme). The change applies instantly across the whole app.
faq.q74: How do I change the app language?
faq.a74: Settings → Language → choose between English, Português and Español. The whole interface updates immediately. Ozly remembers your choice across sessions.
faq.q75: Which notifications can I enable or disable?
faq.a75: Settings → Notifications. You can toggle: Morning Reminder (start your day), Friday Summary (weekly recap), Weekly Summary, Expense Reminder (don't forget to log) and Job Reminders (before each shift). Each one is independent.
faq.q76: How do I connect or disconnect Google Calendar?
faq.a76: Settings → Integrations → Google Calendar → Connect / Disconnect. After connecting, choose which calendars to sync. When connected, you can import events as job suggestions and Ozly will avoid duplicates.
faq.q77: How do I customise email and SMS templates?
faq.a77: Settings → Message Templates. You'll find three editable templates: Invoice Send, Invoice Reminder and Reimbursement. Use placeholders like {name}, {number}, {amount}, {date} that fill in automatically when you send. Tap Reset to Default to restore the original.
faq.q78: How do I earn XP in the Hustle Score?
faq.a78: You earn XP by completing actions in the app: Complete a job +20 XP · Generate an invoice +50 XP · Mark invoice as paid +100 XP · Log an expense +5 XP · Daily streak +10 XP/day · Successful referral +500 XP · Leave a review +100 XP.
faq.q79: How do I level up my tier?
faq.a79: There are 4 tiers: Starter (0 XP) → Hustler (1,000 XP) → Pro (5,000 XP) → Legend (15,000 XP). Each level-up triggers a celebration with confetti and unlocks a new theme accent colour you can apply in Settings.
faq.q80: I'm not getting notifications. What can I do?
faq.a80: Check three things: (1) Settings → Notifications inside Ozly — make sure they're enabled; (2) iOS/Android system settings → Ozly → Notifications enabled; (3) battery optimisation — disable battery saver for Ozly so background notifications can fire.
faq.q81: How much does each plan cost?
faq.a81: TFN: AUD $12.50/month on annual plan ($14.99/month monthly) — for employees, includes Net Salary Forecast, Tax Thermometer, Visa Shield, Reimbursements. ABN: AUD $12.50/month on annual plan ($14.99/month monthly) — for contractors, includes Invoices, Contractors, Tax Reports, Expense OCR. Pro: AUD $16.67/month on annual plan ($19.99/month monthly) — everything from TFN + ABN, plus 3-way Toggle, Consolidated Dashboard and Rate Comparison. All include a 14-day free trial with no card required.
faq.q82: How do I restore my purchases on a new phone?
faq.a82: Open Ozly and go to Paywall → Restore Purchases (or Settings → Subscription → Restore). Sign in with the same Apple ID or Google Account you used to subscribe and your plan reactivates automatically.
faq.q83: How do I upgrade or downgrade my plan?
faq.a83: On iOS: Settings app → Apple ID → Subscriptions → Ozly → choose new plan. On Android: Play Store → Subscriptions → Ozly → Manage. Changes take effect at the next billing cycle (downgrades) or immediately (upgrades).
faq.q84: How do I see when the app last synced?
faq.a84: Open the drawer (☰) — at the bottom you'll see Last sync: [date/time]. To force a manual sync, pull-to-refresh on the Dashboard or Jobs screen, or tap the sync icon in the drawer.
faq.q85: What happens if there's a data conflict between devices?
faq.a85: If the same record was edited offline on two devices, Ozly applies a last-write-wins rule using the modification timestamp. The most recent edit prevails. You'll see a small badge if a conflict was resolved, so you can review and re-edit if needed.
faq.q86: How do I report a bug or contact support?
faq.a86: Send an email to support@ozly.au with: app version (Settings → About), device, OS version, the steps to reproduce the issue, and a screenshot or screen recording if possible. We respond within 1–2 business days.
faq.q87: Which devices and OS versions does Ozly support?
faq.a87: iOS: iPhone running iOS 14 or later. Android: Android 8 (API 26) or later. iPad and tablets work but the layout is optimised for phones. Apple Watch and Wear OS are not supported yet.
faq.q88: Can I use Ozly outside Australia?
faq.a88: Ozly is designed specifically for the Australian tax and visa system (ATO, Department of Home Affairs). You can install and use it abroad, but the tax calculations, GST rules, penalty rates and Visa Shield are all Australia-specific. There are no plans to localise it to other countries at the moment.
faq.q89: How do I export all my data (backup)?
faq.a89: Go to Settings → Account → Export My Data. You'll receive an email with a ZIP file containing all your jobs, invoices, expenses, contractors, reimbursements and profile data in CSV and JSON formats — useful for personal backup or as a GDPR/LGPD data portability request.
